_about: Data-driven tier policy for COORD-029 (TOKEN_ECONOMICS.md lever #4). Maps a ticket's resolved tier to (a) a suggested model class for dispatch routing and (b) tier-appropriate evidence-depth minimums enforced by gov doctor / move-review. SAFETY CONTRACT: a tier may only RELAX evidence depth BELOW the current flat minimum; it can never weaken the `critical` or `standard` tiers. `standard` and `critical` use the sentinel "today" so doctor keeps TODAY's behavior byte-identical for them; only lower tiers (e.g. `mechanical`) supply a smaller numeric minimum. The flat (pre-tier) minimum is review cycles = 4 on repo-backed product tickets / 3 on coordination (repo X) tickets, at least 1 feature-proof and at least 2 critical invariants on product tickets. Edit policy here; never hardcode it in governance.js.

derivation._note: When a ticket has no explicit board `Tier` column value, its tier is derived from its priority (Pri) using this map; an unmatched/absent priority falls back to default_tier. This mapping is documentation-grade and intentionally conservative: only the lowest priorities derive to `mechanical`.

mechanical._note: Mechanical/low-risk work: a smaller model and relaxed evidence depth. Relaxations only ever apply to this lower tier.

standard._note: Safe default and the back-compat anchor: `today` means use the existing flat minimum unchanged, so existing/standard tickets keep byte-identical behavior.

critical._note: Safety-critical work: full rigor, never weakened. Uses the frontier model class and today's full evidence minimums.
